abstract: Traditional dependency parsers typically rely on millions of sparse indicator features, which often suffer from poor generalization and high computational overhead, significantly limiting parsing speed. In this paper, we introduce a novel neural network classifier designed for greedy, transition-based dependency parsing. By learning and utilizing a compact set of dense feature representations, our approach achieves high efficiency while delivering a 2% improvement in both labeled and unlabeled attachment scores across English and Chinese benchmarks. Our implementation is highly performant, processing over 1,000 sentences per second while maintaining a 92.2% unlabeled attachment score on the English Penn Treebank.
